replies.text: +Colin Hammill which process mate?

replies.author: @hammysmad
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith Of submitting and getting a product certified CE boss. The search results I got laid more emphasis on where it`s made and a safe workplace than the product`s safety. It left you still not knowing how to go about it.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Colin Hammill yep - that's what they do - if it is a product it has to come off the production line as a sample

author: @timothyford1534
text: Robert Murray-Smith! This is brilliant! I have had a nagging problem with an LED lighting array for my Mrs.'s mother. She had new cabinets installed in her kitchen and there are LED accent lights, one in the top of each cabinet. The problem is that she   wants them hardwired in and will absolutely not have the potentiometer dimmer dial installed yet wants it to be on a dimmer switch. Sigh... Okay the driver for the array has an input value of 120 volts and um.. at the moment I can't remember the amps. The output to the lights is a value of 12 volts DC at 2 amps. She is fine with the idea of a slider for the dimmer but an on/off switch that has more of a home type feeling to it and not so much of an automotive or industrial feel to it that will run on the low voltage has been tricky. I'm almost certain I can find a touch sensor that runs on those specs and I can just wire the slider switch in series between the paint and the lights and that should take care of it. Funny thing about all of this is that I have a quart of conductive paint on hand already. I even just used some of it to strain the black sand out of the paint to use for another project! I have plenty left.. Now to just find the right touch sensor.... Hey thanks again Robert!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: no worries mate - any chance of a video when you do it - i'd love to see

replies.author: @timothyford1534
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith absolutely! Granted you'll have to be a bit patient with me. My Mrs. and I and our parents live in northern states. One pair of her Grandparents go southward for each winter and I happen to have been born in the state they migrate to. So I too have family there. This past holiday season has been pretty harsh on our elderly family members all around with their health. She lost two distant relatives toward the end of the year and spent several days in the hospital and at several different intervals with both of her Grandfathers starting before Thanksgiving and we'll just say at the moment no one is in the hospital but we are not out of the woods yet. Given the reprieve we decided to go southbound to try and spend some quality time with the family we have down there.. Kinda one of those just in case type things. Plus we needed a break from the hustle and bustle of everything up north. When we return I will surely get down to business with that awesome switch idea. Its one of the last things that need done to finish her kitchen remodel project. I know that she will be real good and happy to have it done. Also this would be my first video I will have ever posted and unfortunately I don't have a dedicated camera to take video on so it will have to be done using my phone. I have a Motorola Droid TURBO which should make the process somewhat better I hope. At 21 mega pixels it takes video in 1080p HD resolution though it also has the capability to record at Ultra HD resolution which is four times the resolution of standard HD. Since I have never posted myself do you have any suggestions or a video you have or may know of that will shed some insight on the best way to get the job done? If its just a fairly standard thing then no worries about answering that. Otherwise thank you in advance for any advice. And to give you an idea of time it will probably be a couple of weeks and it will be done. I'll send you a message and let you know that it's been done I'm going to order the sensor online so it will be there waiting on me to try and lessen the delay. Again thanks for your efforts to share and educate the masses. It really made my day and relieved me of one of my headaches for sure!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
Timothy Ford
sorry to hear about your problems mate - videoing is super easy and the phone should do just fine - it's like anything scary at first and easier after a few - good luck with it mate

author: @TheGEODEAHOLIC
text: ingenious, & if one didn't mind stringing some wire around one could activate lamps etc from multiple points, very handy for stairways etc.  with a little more effort one could make the switch 'U' shaped such that one could activate it without actually touching it, which wood be very usefull for hospitals & germophobes.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I am going to try swapping my house switches out for this - i'll do a video on it

author: @noworldorderforme
text: It's great to have you back in Blighty and posting thought provoking videos again Robert. I could see this idea being combined with your wall heater design to make an easily fabricated 'touch the wall and it generates heat' system. It would make a great low cost, solid state heating solution for domestic situations. If you could formulate an electroluminescent paint add-on indication to remind the user that the device was in operation, that would make it perfect. There are a few electroluminescent paint systems coming onto the market, but I'm sure that you could come up with a better, cheaper product given your obvious genius for this sort of applied chemistry. Cheers.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that is a nice idea mate - i like it - if you used the touch switch to activate a relay you could handle any load whatsoever.
